title: SherpaCarta Canada Digital Rights Petition
subtitle: Adopt 114 articles as model law — starting in British Columbia

jurisdiction.scope: All signatures are Canadian civic commitments. BC is our first provincial beachhead within Canada.

petitionText: We, residents and citizens of Canada, petition the House of Commons, the Government of Canada, and the Legislative Assembly of British Columbia to adopt SherpaCarta — the Global Digital Magna Carta — as model legislation for digital human rights, including but not limited to: absolute privacy and prohibition of mass surveillance (Art. 11), full data sovereignty (Art. 12), prohibition of surveillance capitalism (Art. 13), right to be forgotten (Art. 47), algorithmic transparency and non-discrimination (Arts. 61–62), and the living charter principle that rights may only expand (Art. 114). We request concrete action to modernize PIPEDA, establish a BC Digital Rights Act, and require Privacy by Design in all public-sector and vendor systems serving Canadians.

federal.name: House of Commons e-Petition
federal.requirements: 500+ validated signatures; MP authorization; name, unique email, province, postal code, phone, email confirmation
federal.note: SherpaCarta never collects this data — sign officially on Parliament's site when our e-petition is live.

bc.name: BC Legislative Assembly Petition
bc.requirements: Paper petition format; presented by MLA

privacy.officialSeparation: Federal and BC official petitions are separate voluntary steps on government websites

share.defaultText: I signed SherpaCarta for Canada — 114 articles protecting digital privacy and data sovereignty. BC leads, Canada follows. Privacy is a birthright. #CanadaBCChallenge #SherpaCarta
